cmes.note: Bright loop CME and shock first seen to the Northeast by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-11T00:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this CME is a long duration C6.7 class flare from Active Region 14465 (N09E39) seen peaking at 2026-06-11T00:02Z. This flare can be seen in SDO AIA 131 along with a wide region of dimming, field line opening and an EUV wave seen in SDO AIA 171, 193, 211 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: UPDATED BULK parameters using later frames in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Updated shock measurement based on the best fit between the outermost wide feature seen in SOHO LASCO C3 imagery and a majority of the widest feature seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery, excluding small portions of the upper and lower sections of the shock feature.
PRELIMINARY BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
PRELIMINARY SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the east/southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an eruption and opening of field lines visible over the eastern limb in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195 imagery starting around 2026-06-11T07:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint, thin CME observed to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. The source is likely associated with a C9.0 flare (N10E34) from AR 14465 peaking at 2026-06-11T08:28Z, and a larger area of dimming centered nearby (N12E28) observed in SDO AIA 193 triggered with the flare.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between STEREO A COR2 which views the front better, and SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the E only in STEREO A COR2. The source is likely dimming in SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-06-11T08:48Z centered around S25W32.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A COR2 only 3D fit using the observed source centered at W32. The front is very faint and difficult to resolve even in running difference imagery, and parameters may vary as a result of this uncertainty, but it does evolve slowly regardless.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the E only in STEREO A COR2, likely due to being occulted by the GOES CCOR-1 and/or SOHO LASCO C2/C3 occulting disks, or missed by a SOHO LASCO C2/C3 downlink delay starting at 2026-06-11T19:36Z. The source may be AR 14465, as a weak filament eruption and field line movement centered around N08E33 is seen starting around 2026-06-11T17:04Z in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A COR2 only 3D analysis using E33 as the likely source longitude.

cmes.note: CME observed to the S in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3, not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap starting at 2026-06-11T19:53Z and not in SOHO LASCO C2 due to a downlink gap between approximately 2026-06-11T19:36Z to 2026-06-11T22:12Z. The source is a far-sided high southern latitude filament eruption observed in GOES SUVI 304 over the S limb at 2026-06-11T19:32Z and on/beyond the SW limb in STEREO A EUVI 304 at 2026-06-11T20:15Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: GOES CCOR-1 plane-of-sky with no STEREO A COR2 to triangulate the far-sided source.

cmes.note: Very narrow, minor CME seen to the southeast in SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2A, and GOES CCOR-1 without a very clear source; may be associated with a narrow eruption seen best in GOES SUVI 284 near 2026-06-12T00:30Z near the eastern limb, however, confidence is fairly low as the CME geometry would suggest a much further southerly latitude than this source.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary leading edge measurement using all available spacecraft and triangulation to fit the source.

cmes.note: Narrow CME seen to the southeast in STEREO A COR2A and very dimly to the due south in SOHO LASCO C2 which is likely associated with an eruption with dimming from AR 14464 near S12W20 around 2026-06-12T13:50Z seen best in SDO AIA composite imagery using the sum of 211/193/171 Angstrom imagers. Also seen in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2A for triangulation, noting the eastward deflection along the Earth-Sun line due to the presence of an equatorial coronal hole near W45.

cmes.note: Faint partial halo CME seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 directed towards the east. The CME narrower with a brighter bulk and disconnected bulk/shock as seen in STEREO A COR2A, with a brighter bulk to the east-northeast and shock extending from the southeast to the north. This CME is associated with a filament eruption near N00E35 erupting near 2026-06-12T16:15Z, best seen in SDO AIA composite imagery using the sum of 211/193/171 Angstroms and as well in GOES SUVI/SDO AIA 304 Angstroms. This CME, seen due east and northeast in COR2A, is seen overlapping the previous CME: 2026-06-12T15:38Z which is seen to the southeast and which is a separate eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement out to 10 solar radii with medium confidence using SOHO/STEREO A/GOES for triangulation; the CME appears to fit closer to the Earth-Sun line than the source at E35 given the halo-like presentation.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the SSE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-12T18:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a filament eruption likely originating from the far side seen in GOES SUVI 304 beginning at approx. 2026-06-12T18:16Z. Faint field line movement can also be seen along the SE limb in SDO AIA 171 in association with this eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Loop CME first seen to the East by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-12T19:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a filament eruption seen along the Eastern limb (approx. S10E80 with some Northern deflection) in SDO AIA /GOES SUVI 304 beginning at approx. 2026-06-12T18:56Z. Wide opening field lines and post eruptive arcades are also visible in SDO AIA 131, 171, 193, 211 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the East beginning at 2026-06-12T23:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap beginning at 2026-06-12T20:23Z. The source of this event is likely continued outflow following a large filament eruption resulting in CME: 2026-06-12T19:24Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky using a longitude of E90 due to lack of clear source and no STEREO A COR2 imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: Narrow, outflow CME first seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-13T03:36Z. This feature is most likely outflow following CME: 2026-06-12T19:24Z as a separate source has not been found. It is also seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based solely on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 as no source could be found.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-06-13T11:01Z. It is also seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. This CME is most likely far-sided as no source candidate was found on the Earth-facing disk. This is consistent with the triangulated measurement.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 stating at 2026-06-13T13:26Z. It is also seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and to the S in STEREO A COR2. The majority of this CME was missed by SOHO LASCO C2/C3 in real time due to a data gap between downlink periods. The source of this CME is a large eruption in across the far SE portion of the Earth-facing disk centered around AR 14461 (S21W68). The eruption is best seen in SDO AIA 193 as wide opening field lines accompanied by dimming and an EUVI wave beginning around 2026-06-13T12:36Z. This CME is also associated with a C2.5 class flare from AR 14461 which peaked at 2026-06-13T13:09Z. The flare is best seen in SDO AIA 131.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 as well as on visible source location.

cmes.note: Narrow, faint CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 around 2026-06-13T17:36Z. Not clearly visible in other coronagraph imagery. No source has been found on the Earth-facing disk. This feature is most likely outflow following CME: 2026-06-13T13:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen because this feature is only seen in SOHO LASCO C2 and does not have a source candidate on the Earth-facing disk.

cmes.note: CME seen to the due north in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 in association with a far-sided eruption occurring near the solar north pole near 2026-06-14T19:00Z, best seen in GOES SUVI 284 as a fast loop with separating field lines over the limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using primarily SOHO LASCO C2 and a few frames of GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the Northeast by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-15T02:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 in later frames. This CME is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 or GOES CCOR-1. The source of this event is likely far sided, as no clear on disk signatures are observed.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of E90 due to far sided source and lack of coronagraph imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: Bright loop CME first seen to the Southwest by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-15T06:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a large, slow prominence eruption beginning at approx. 2026-06-15T03:00Z, with liftoff beginning at 2026-06-15T06:00Z as seen along the SW limb in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304. Field line opening can also be seen in SDO AIA 171, 193 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the Southwest by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-15T07:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is faint field line opening seen along the SW limb in SDO AIA 171 in ISWA beginning at approx. 2026-06-15T07:00Z. This CME heavily overlaps with CME: 2026-06-15T06:36Z which is related to a large prominence eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3/STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint, narrow CME only visible to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2. The source is filament ejecta from Active Region 14464 (S11W78) best seen in GOES SUVI 304 starting around 2026-06-16T10:52Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and the potential source location. Adjusted to possible directionality based on later STEREO A COR2 frames used in the measurement of CME: 2026-06-16T16:00Z which was from the same source.

cmes.note: Faint, narrow CME only visible to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2. The source is filament ejecta from Active Region 14464 (S11W78) best seen in GOES SUVI 304 starting around 2026-06-16T13:12Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and the potential source location. Adjusted to possible directionality based on later STEREO A COR2 frames used in the measurement of CME: 2026-06-16T16:00Z which was from the same source.

cmes.note: Faint CME to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2, C3, CCOR-1, and to the S/SW in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is a filament eruption/ejecta from Active Region 14464 (S11W78) as seen in SDO/AIA and GOES SUVI wavelengths starting around 2026-06-16T15:24Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW by GOES CCOR-1 beginning at 2026-06-16T20:45Z. This CME is not clearly seen in other coronagraphs. The source of this CME is a narrow filament eruption from AR 14464 (S14W83) beginning at approx. 2026-06-16T20:30Z as seen in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304 exhibiting strong Southern deflection.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in STEREO A COR2 imagery and can be seen faintly and partially obscured by the pylon GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in SOHO imagery in real-time due to a data gap starting at 2026-06-16T18:42Z. The source is far-sided and is likely related to a filament eruption originating from beyond the northeast limb in GOES SUVI 304 imagery starting around 2026-06-16T18:45Z. Faint field line movement is also visible in SDO AIA 171, 193 and GOES SUVI 284 imagery around this time.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using STEREO A COR2 imagery with a longitude of -29 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location. This event was faint and blocked by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 imagery, which did not allow for a reliable triangulated measurement.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the west in GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery or in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery due to a real-time data gap from 2026-06-16T18:36Z to 2026-06-17T14:12Z. The source is an eruption from Active Region 14464 (~S14W90) starting around 2026-06-17T12:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an eruption from Active Region 14464 (~S14W90) starting around 2026-06-17T15:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-17T17:48Z. It is also seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. This is the first in a series of three CMEs. The source is an eruption from Active Region 14464 (~S14W96) starting around 2026-06-17T17:30Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 as well as on visible source.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-17T18:48Z. It is also seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is not seen in STEREO A COR2, likely because it is obscured by the occulting disk. This is the second in a series of three CMEs and over takes the first CME: 2026-06-17T18:00Z. The source of this CME is a small filament eruption over the NW limb best seen in SDO AIA 304 starting as early as 2026-06-17T18:33Z. A small eruption can be seen in STEREO A EUVI 195 from the vicinity of former Active Region 14463 (~N17W93) beginning around 2026-06-17T18:35Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best-fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 as well as on visible source location.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-06-17T20:24Z. It is also seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is not seen in STEREO A COR2, likely because it is obscured by the occulting disk. This is the third in a series of three CMEs. The source of this CME is most likely a small eruption from beyond the W limb from the vicinity of former AR 14464. Ejecta can be seen in SDO AIA 304 starting around 2026-06-17T19:48Z, and the eruption can be seen in STEREO A EUVI 195 beginning around 2026-06-17T19:55Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 as well as on visible source location.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-06-18T:00Z. It is also seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is not seen in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely a small filament eruption from beyond the NW limb best seen in SDO AIA 304 beginning around 2026-06-17T23:24Z, likely from former AR 14463 (N17W96).

cmeAnalyses.note: PRELIMINARY measurement based on limited imagery and probably source location.
